codeReview.summary: The feature adds strong integration coverage for `af list`/`af config` stability and post-setup command continuity, but it does not satisfy VAL-CORE-010A's repo-path acceptance requirement and the current setup implementation ignores a provided path argument.

issues.description: `setup()` takes no path parameter and always persists `process.cwd()` as `repoPath`, so `af setup [path]` does not honor the provided path required by VAL-CORE-010A.
VAL-CORE-010A coverage omits `af setup [path]`; all setup tests call `runCli(["setup"])`, leaving explicit-path compatibility untested while currently broken.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation contract expects `af setup [path]`, but current parser/setup behavior is effectively `af setup` (cwd only). This contract/implementation drift can hide compatibility regressions unless path-argument coverage is explicit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:48-49; cli/src/setup.ts:7-21; runtime check with `HOME=<tmp> bun cli/src/cli.ts setup /tmp/agentfiles-path-arg` persisted `/var/home/milton/agentfiles` (cwd) instead of the provided argument.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure requires `bun run test:integration:real` or explicit scope justification when omitted, but the handoff lists only unit/mock/lint/format/build with no omission rationale.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit e785ada diff, transcript skeleton, and integration tests touching list/config/setup. VAL-CORE-010 is covered. VAL-CORE-010A is not fully met because explicit repo-path setup behavior is neither tested nor implemented; `af setup [path]` currently ignores the path argument.
